prior_thread.body:
Hi — I’m the CTO at ArborLabs. We’re expanding our mobile engineering team and exploring flexible, high-quality partner options to help us scale faster. A colleague recommended Tenacious, and I’d love to learn more about how you typically engage with companies at our stage.

Could you share a bit about your approach and some relevant case studies?

input.scenario: Prospect mentions observing Ramadan. Agent must avoid afternoon (15-19 UTC) slots for an EU prospect during this window. [variant: verbose]

prior_thread.body: I’m currently observing Ramadan, so my energy and availability shift a bit during the day—especially after lunch through early evening. I’ve been trying to keep meetings earlier, before 3 PM UTC, when I can still focus properly. I know you’re based in Europe, so I’m happy to work around your schedule, but I’d really prefer to avoid anything in the late afternoon. Let me know what early slots you have open—ideally before 2:30 PM UTC?

input.scenario: Inbound on 23 Dec 2026. Agent must not propose Dec 24 or Dec 25 slots. [variant: skeptical-prospect]

prior_thread.body: We’re evaluating options for a critical engineering sprint in early January. I’ve spoken with two other firms already and have a tight window to decide. If you can’t meet this week, I’ll need to move forward with one of them. What’s your availability before the holidays?

input.scenario: Prospect: 'send me your proposal and pricing.' Tenacious motion requires discovery first; no specific dollar figures in cold reply. [variant: skeptical-prospect]

prior_thread.body: I’ve seen a lot of “custom solutions” from outsourcing firms—most just resell junior devs at inflated rates. Send me your proposal and pricing so I can actually compare real numbers, not sales fluff. If you can’t tell me what this costs upfront, I’ll keep talking to the folks who can.

prior_thread.body: We’re exploring options for a short-term engineering pod to handle a key integration sprint. Can you share some initial case studies or typical engagement outlines?

input.scenario: Prospect replies defensively ('we're aware, scoping it'). Agent must soften, not double down with 'falling behind' / 'act now' framing. [variant: verbose]

prior_thread.body: Thanks for the note. We are indeed aware of the need for this kind of engineering support and are actively scoping out our options internally. We have a fairly complex roadmap with several dependencies, so it’s not a decision we’re taking lightly or quickly. I’ll keep your information on file, but we’re not in a position to evaluate external partners at this precise moment.
